per_turn_leaks.student_message: Eight to ten times a day is a dramatic increase. With that frequency, are the symptoms disrupting your sleep? Do you wake up at night needing to go?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Actually, yeah... I do wake up at night sometimes. It's not every night, but 2-3 times a week I'll wake up and need to rush to the bathroom. It's really been affecting my sleep quality.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Nocturnal diarrhea is a red flag — it suggests an organic rather than functional cause. With that many bowel movements, do you feel like you're fully emptying each time, or is there a sensation of incomplete evacuation?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Honestly, doc, I feel like I'm not even close to being done after most bowel movements. It's like my body just can't seem to get rid of everything, and it leaves me feeling really uncomfortable and anxious about when the next episode will hit.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tenesmus with bloody diarrhea points strongly toward an inflammatory process in the rectum and colon. With this level of GI inflammation, the body often responds systemically. Have you had any fevers or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, doc, I've been running a low-grade fever off and on for a few weeks now. It's not too bad, but it's definitely there - my temperature is usually around 98.6 to 99 degrees Fahrenheit. And sometimes, when I'm having one of those really bad diarrhea episodes, I'll feel like I'm getting chills, but it's not a full-blown chill, if that makes sense.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Knee and ankle pain — those are classic large joint manifestations of IBD. Are you taking anything for the joint pain? Any over-the-counter medications?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yeah... I take ibuprofen about 2-3 times a week for the knee and ankle pain, but it actually seems to make my abdominal symptoms worse, which is weird. And I've also been drinking more beer than usual lately, trying to calm my nerves with the stress of work and dealing with these symptoms, but I'm not sure if that's making things better or worse.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Ibuprofen is important to note — NSAIDs can actually worsen inflammatory bowel disease. That might be contributing to your flare. With bloody diarrhea eight to ten times a day for three weeks, you must be losing weight. Have you noticed any changes on the scale?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yeah... I've definitely lost some weight over the past few weeks. I'm not sure exactly how much, but it feels like I've dropped around 9 pounds or so. It's been really frustrating because I've always been pretty active and now I just feel tired all the time from dealing with these symptoms.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Increased alcohol use as a coping mechanism — I understand, but alcohol can also irritate the GI tract and worsen inflammation. Let me ask about family history — inflammatory bowel disease has a strong genetic component. Has anyone in your family had Crohn's disease, ulcerative colitis, or similar conditions?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Doc, yeah... my older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease when she was 30 years old. She's been dealing with it for a while now and has to take medication regularly to manage her symptoms. It's definitely made me worried that I might be at risk too.
